titleStmt.title: Berlin, Berlin State Library, Petermann II Nachtrag 42
Isaiah, Jeremiah Cycle, Ezekiel

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.title: Book of Isaiah
msItem.note: Isaiah 40:7-45 (expected between and ) is missing. contains Isaiah 41:25-42; contains Isaiah 44:13-24.

Valid for the entire manuscript: According to Dillmann, one of the oldest, least exact and more corrupt versions of the text, with the old division into sections and several rare text parts not present anymore in more recent manuscripts.

The text is divided into 16 "visions" (ራእይ፡, beginning at Is 1:1, 2:1, 6:1, 7:1, 13:1, 14:28, 15:1, 16:13, 17:1, 19:1, 20:1, 21:1, 21:11, 22:1, 23:1, 30:6), the "Prayer of Isaiah" (Is 26:9) and the "Prayer of Hezekiah" (Is 38:9). Is 36:1 and 38:1 are also highlighted by rubrication of the first lines.

msItem.title: Cycle of Jeremiah
msItem.note: The following beginnings are rubricated, but not indicated in the margin: Jer 11:1, 13:8, 14:1, 15:1, 18:1, 25:1 (wrongly indicated as 25:14 in the text), 26:1, 26:7, 28:12, 30:1, 44:15, 46:1, 47:1, 46:13 (as 46:13-24 is located between 47:3 and 4), 48:1, 48:36, 49:1, 49:7, 49:28, 49:34, 50:1, 52:6, 52:12.
The titles ዳግም፡, ሣልስ፡ and ራብዕ፡ ዜማ፡ ዘእምሰቆቃው፡ are indicated in red in the upper margin of the second, third and fourth lamentations.

msItem.title: Prophecy of Jeremiah Told to Pashhur
msItem.note: The text of the Prophecy of Jeremiah Told to Pashhur is part of the Book of Jeremiah between Jer. 20:3-4.

msItem.title: Book of Baruch
msItem.note: This work is not set apart by layout from the preceding .

msItem.title: The Rest of the Words of Baruch
msItem.explicit: ወትራፋተ፡ ነገሩሰ፡ ለእርምያስ፡ ነዋ፡ ጽሑፍ፡ ውስተ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ባሮክ። ወተፈጸመ፡ ዘባሮክ፨

msItem.title: Book of Ezekiel - Eth I
msItem.note: From chapter 42 on, the text is much abbreviated, as in other old manuscripts of Ezekiel.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation in the outer upper corner of each recto. Two paper guard leaves each at the beginning and end of the manuscript, part of the European binding, have not been foliated and are referred to here as .

collation.note: A quaternion is missing between and , of which two folia are located at and . should be located before .
collation.signatures: Undecorated quire signatures in red and black in the upper inner corner of the first leaf of to and to . The quire numeration starts anew with , with the first quire mark being ፪ on .

list.item:
5
1, stub after 5 2, stub after 5 3, stub after 5

item.note: The leaves of the last quire are all mounted on guards, the original quire structure is not discernible anymore.

supportDesc.condition: Partly damaged by humidity, dirt and animals. The lower margin of and the outer margins of are cut off.

layout.note: Number of characters per line per column: 10-11.
Data on text area and margin dimensions taken from .
layout.ab: Vertical pricks are visible; horizontal pricks are not visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling; the lower line is written above the ruling.

layout.note: Number of characters per line per column: 11-13.
Data on text area and margin dimensions taken from .
layout.ab: Vertical pricks are visible; horizontal pricks are not visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling; the lower line is written above the ruling.

handNote.desc: Large, archaic characters, not very correct.

decoNote.desc: Full-page polychrome arabesque frame with headpiece, see A Handlist of Illustrated Early Solomonic Manuscripts in British Public CollectionsAethiopicaJonasKarlssonJacopoGnisciSophiaDege-Müller202326159-225https://journals.sub.uni-hamburg.de/aethiopica/article/view/2106.

item.desc: The manuscript contains many erasures and corrections above erasures, sometimes in condensed script, throughout.
Note in German on the front pastedown, presumably giving two dates on which the manuscript has been collated, and a signature.
Bookbinder's stamp in the lower outer edge of the paper guard leaf.
item.q: W. Collin Kaiserl. u. Königl. Hofbuchbinder Berlin.

item.desc: Note listing the main content of the manuscript.
item.q: ዝ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ኢሳይያስ፡ ወኤርምያስ፡ ወባሮክ፡ ወሕዝቅኤል።

item.desc: Crude note written below the end of the main text.
item.q: ይትባርክ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ አምላከ፡ እስርኤል፡ አምላከ ለኩሉ፡ መንፈስ፡ እግዚ፡ ወለኵሉ፡ ዘስጋ፡

item.desc: Note written, probably in pencil, in the upper part of the page, partly illegible.
Erasures and corrections by a more recent hand in a few places.
A more recent hand has noted the European chapter numbers (ምዕራፍ፡) in the margins, denotating their place in the text with a cross and occasionally another mark within the text.

binding.decoNote: European binding. The manuscript has been re-bound by the library. The binding includes paper pastedowns and two paper guard leaves each at the beginning and end. Content and shelfmark of the manuscript are impressed in gold on the spine.

history.origin:
The manuscript is not dated, its palaeography makes a production in the
end of the fifteenth century likely. On the relevance of the decoration to the dating, see also A Handlist of Illustrated Early Solomonic Manuscripts in British Public CollectionsAethiopicaJonasKarlssonJacopoGnisciSophiaDege-Müller202326159-225https://journals.sub.uni-hamburg.de/aethiopica/article/view/2106.
history.provenance: Verzeichniss der abessinischen Handschriften, mit drei TafelnDie Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu BerlinAugustDillmannBerlinBuchdruckerei der Königl. Akademie der Wissenschaften (G. Vogt)1878Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin33https://archive.org/details/diehandschriften03preuii indicates that the manuscript was acquired by Julius Heinrich Petermann at some time during his travels in the Levant and Egypt, possibly during his stay as vice consul in Jerusalem in 1868.
history.acquisition: The manuscript was bought by the then Königliche Bibliothek zu Berlin in 1873.

bibl.title: Towards a Critical Edition of the Ethiopic Book of Jeremiah: A Sample Collation of Jer. 1–3 and First Results
Studies on the Ethiopic Old Testament. Prolegomena to a Critical Edition of the Jeremiah Cycle
Supplement to Aethiopica. International Journal of Ethiopian and Eritrean Studies

series.t:title: Supplement to Aethiopica. International Journal of Ethiopian and Eritrean Studies

bibl.title: A Handlist of Illustrated Early Solomonic Manuscripts in British Public Collections
Aethiopica

መጽሐፈ፡ ኢሳይያስ፡ ነቢይ። Der Prophet Jesaia nach der Bibeluebersetzung auf Grund Handschriftlicher Quellen herausgegeben von Dr. J.B., <hi rend="italics">I:</hi> Der aethiopische Text. Mit einem photographischen Specimen des Cod. Aeth. Berol. Peterm. II. Nachtr. 42
The Ethiopic Version of the Lives of the Prophets: Ezekiel and Daniel
Bulletin of the School of Oriental and African Studies
The Ethiopic Version of the Lives of the Prophets, II: Isaiah, Jeremiah, Haggai, Zechariah, Malachi, Elijah, Elisha, Nathan, Ahijah, and Joel
Bulletin of the School of Oriental and African Studies, University of London

Die aethiopische Bibelübersetzung. Ihre Herkunft, Art, Geschichte und ihr Wert für die alt- und neutestamentliche Wissenschaft. Mit Jeremia Cap. 1–13 als Textprobe, dem aeth. Pseudepigraph: die Prophetie des Jeremia an Pashur und einem General-Katalog der abessinischen Handschriften. (Als Prolegomena zu einer kritischen Ausgabe der aeth. Bibel.). I. Bibelkritische Abhandlung: Die Prophetie des Jeremia an Pashur, mit deutscher Übersetzung
Aethiopische Lesestücke. Inedita Aethiopica für den Gebrauch in Universitäts-Vorlesungen
ሰቆቃወ፡ ኤርምያስ፡ ነቢይ። (Saqoqāwa ʾErmǝyās nabiy) Die Klagelieder Jeremiae in der aethiopischen Bibelübersetzung. Auf Grund handschriftlicher Quellen mit text-kritischen Anmerkungen
Ricerche sugli apocrifi veterotestamentari etiopici. I. Sulla Vorlage aramaica dell'Enoch etiopico. II. La traduzione etiopica dei Paralipomeni di Geremia: testo critico con introduzione e commento
The Ethiopic Text of the Book of Ezekiel: A Critical Edition
Verzeichniss der abessinischen Handschriften, mit drei Tafeln
Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin
bibl.publisher: Buchdruckerei der Königl. Akademie der Wissenschaften (G. Vogt)

series.t:title: Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

revisionDesc.change: prepared XML file importing information from SBB oriental digital (http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de), orientalischen Handschriften der Staatsbibliothek zu Berlin – Preußischer Kulturbesitz . http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00025169?XSL.Style=native and related entries: http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059166?XSL.Style=native , http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059167?XSL.Style=native , http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059168?XSL.Style=native , http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059169?XSL.Style=native , http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059170?XSL.Style=native , .
Encoded information provided by Dillmann's catalogue
Updated weight, quire structure and text area measurements after consultation of the manuscript in the library.
